  "text": "The Rust trait solver obligation chain is implemented across several files in the compiler/rustc_data_structures and compiler/rustc_trait_selection crates.\n\n## 1. ObligationForest\n\nThe `ObligationForest` is implemented in `compiler/rustc_data_structures/src/obligation_forest/mod.rs`. It is a utility data structure for tracking the set of outstanding trait obligations. It holds `nodes` (a list of obligations), supports registration of new obligations via `register_obligation`, and processes them via `process_obligations`. The graphviz visualization is in `graphviz.rs` and tests are in `tests.rs`.\n\n## 2. Fulfillment Context\n\nThe `FulfillmentContext` in `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/traits/fulfill.rs` uses `ObligationForest<PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>>` to track all pending obligations during type checking. It drives the main obligation processing loop.\n\n## 3. Trait Selection Candidate Collection\n\nCandidate collection is in `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/traits/select/candidate_assembly.rs`. Functions like `assemble_candidates_from_impls`, `assemble_candidates_for_trait_alias`, `assemble_candidates_for_fn_ptr_trait`, etc., collect all possible ways a trait obligation could be fulfilled into a `SelectionCandidateSet`. The candidate types are defined in `compiler/rustc_middle/src/traits/select.rs` as the `SelectionCandidate` enum (with variants like `SizedCandidate`, `ImplCandidate`, `ParamCandidate`, `CoroutineCandidate`, etc.).\n\nThe main `SelectionContext` struct and its `select` method are in `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/traits/select/mod.rs`.\n\n## 4. Impl Matching\n\nImpl matching is done in `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/traits/select/_match.rs` via `match_impl`, which checks whether a particular impl matches a given trait obligation by unifying the impl's self type and trait arguments with those of the obligation. This is called from `candidate_assembly.rs` when probing whether an `ImplCandidate` applies, and from `confirmation.rs` via `rematch_impl` when confirming a selected candidate.\n\nThe confirmation phase is in `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/traits/select/confirmation.rs`, with `confirm_candidate` dispatching to specific confirmation routines like `confirm_impl_candidate`, which calls `rematch_impl` and then `vtable_impl` to generate the nested obligations.\n\n## 5. Error Reporting for Unsatisfied Trait Bounds\n\nError reporting lives in `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/`, with the main file being `fulfillment_errors.rs`. This contains functions to report `FulfillmentError`s (unsatisfied trait bounds) with helpful messages, suggestions, and notes. The `on_unimplemented.rs` handles the `#[rustc_on_unimplemented]` attribute for custom error messages. The `suggestions.rs` provides code fix suggestions. For the new trait solver, `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/solve/fulfill/derive_errors.rs` contains `fulfillment_error_for_no_solution` which derives fulfillment errors from the new solver's proof tree."
}

### 1. ObligationForest
- **`compiler/rustc_data_structures/src/obligation_forest/mod.rs`** — Core `ObligationForest<O>` data structure. Tracks all outstanding trait obligations, with `register_obligation` and `process_obligations` as the main entry points.
- **`graphviz.rs`** and **`tests.rs`** in the same directory support visualization and testing.

### 2. Fulfillment Context (bridges ObligationForest to trait solving)
- **`compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/traits/fulfill.rs`** — `FulfillmentContext` wraps `ObligationForest<PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>>` and drives obligation processing during type checking.

### 3. Trait Selection Candidate Collection
- **`compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/traits/select/mod.rs`** — `SelectionContext`, the main driver for trait selection.
- **`compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/traits/select/candidate_assembly.rs`** — Functions like `assemble_candidates_from_impls`, `assemble_candidates_for_trait_alias`, etc. collect all possible `SelectionCandidate`s.
- **`compiler/rustc_middle/src/traits/select.rs`** — Defines `SelectionCandidate` enum and `SelectionCache`.

### 4. Impl Matching
- **`compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/traits/select/_match.rs`** — `match_impl` unifies an impl's self-type/trait-args with an obligation's types.
- **`compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/traits/select/confirmation.rs`** — `confirm_candidate` / `confirm_impl_candidate` confirm the chosen candidate and generate nested obligations.

### 5. Error Reporting for Unsatisfied Trait Bounds
- **`compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/fulfillment_errors.rs`** — Main error reporting for unsatisfied trait bounds.
- **`suggestions.rs`**, **`on_unimplemented.rs`**, **`ambiguity.rs`**, **`overflow.rs`** — Supporting error reporting modules.
- **`compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/solve/fulfill/derive_errors.rs`** — New solver's error derivation (`fulfillment_error_for_no_solution`).
